applied_optimizations.name: torch.set_float32_matmul_precision('high')
applied_optimizations.description: Enable TF32 tensor cores for float32 matrix multiplication on MI355X, significantly speeding up the GEMM operation in the Linear layer

applied_optimizations.name: Triton fused LogSumExp + activations kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Fused kernel that combines LogSumExp reduction with LeakyReLU x2 and GELU x2 into a single Triton kernel, reducing memory bandwidth and kernel launch overhead. Uses manual tanh implementation for ROCm compatibility.

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile wrapping ModelNew
attempted_but_failed.reason: Incompatibility between torch.compile's triton kernel wrapping and custom Triton version

notes: After adding torch.set_float32_matmul_precision('high'), both reference and optimized models improved significantly (reference: 0.365ms, optimized: 0.309ms)
The Triton kernel fuses LogSumExp + 4 elementwise ops, saving kernel launches
BLOCK_SIZE = n_cols (8192) works well for MI355X wavefront size 64
torch.compile wrapping the model with Triton kernel causes correctness issues due to triton version incompatibility

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Third consecutive trial with no output. Agent appears stuck and not reaching the benchmark execution step. Providing extremely explicit, copy-paste-ready instructions to make a single targeted change

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 5 produced no output — the 4th consecutive failure. The agent is consistently getting stuck before running the benchmark. Providing the absolute minimum set of instructions: a single sed command

global_notes: MI355X (gfx950, CDNA4) has wavefront size 64, not 32 like NVIDIA
tl.math.tanh is unavailable on ROCm Triton - must use manual implementation
The GEMM operation dominates runtime; elementwise fusion provides modest but meaningful improvement
